title: -z duration flag ignores -n but still requires -c >= 1
body: When -z is set, num is forced to math.MaxInt32 in hey.go's main(), and the code exits with '-c cannot be smaller than 1.' if conc <= 0, but the usage text never explains that -n is silently overridden. Requests just run until duration is reached, which surprised me when I passed both -n 100 -z 10s expecting only 100 requests.

title: Deprecated -h header flag error message doesn't mention -H alternative clearly enough
body: If you pass -h 'Accept: text/html' by habit from curl, hey.go calls usageAndExit("Flag '-h' is deprecated, please use '-H' instead.") but flag.Usage() then reprints the entire usage block, burying the actual deprecation message. Would be clearer to print just the deprecation notice without the full usage dump.

title: QueryPerformanceCounter timing on Windows can drift under high concurrency
body: now_windows.go computes now() via QueryPerformanceCounter divided by a frequency captured once at startup (qpcFrequency). On some Hyper-V guests the TSC-based QPC frequency can change after VM migration, which would skew every duration/offset measurement in result. Should we re-query the frequency periodically or document this limitation?

title: Add JSON output option alongside existing CSV
body: requester/print.go currently only supports '-o csv' for dumping per-request metrics; everything else goes through the human-readable summary. It would help scripting/CI pipelines to add an '-o json' mode that emits the same fields as CSV but as structured JSON lines.

title: Proxy URL parsing accepts malformed schemes without validation
body: In hey.go, proxyURL is built with gourl.Parse(*proxyAddr) but there's no check that the scheme is http/https/socks5 before it's passed into requester.Work.ProxyAddr and used by requester.go's client transport. Passing something like '-x notaurl' currently produces a proxy with an empty scheme instead of a clear error.
